ogTitle: Lansdowne Forest vs Lapstone: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lansdowne Forest and Lapstone.
heading: Lansdowne Forest vs Lapstone — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lansdowne Forest, New South Wales and Lapstone, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lansdowne Forest has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Lapstone (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lansdowne Forest or Lapstone safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lansdowne Forest has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Lapstone scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lansdowne Forest scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Lapstone scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lansdowne Forest or Lapstone?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lansdowne Forest has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
